title: centaurea scopulorum var. gracilior
intro: Centaurea scopulorum var. gracilior, also known as a type of knapweed, is a flowering plant in the sunflower family that is native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are used to treat a range of ailments.
